issuesFound.fix: Line 2140: function _syncToggleState(period) { if (!_periodBtns) return; _periodBtns.forEach(btn => { const btnPeriod = btn.getAttribute('data-period'); const isActive = btnPeriod === period; btn.setAttribute('aria-pressed', String(isActive)); toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); });

issuesFound.title: breadcrumb last item missing aria-current

issuesFound.title: suggestionListRenderer creates <a> without href
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestion list renderer creates an <a> element for suggestions even if they do not have a destination URL (href). An <a> without an href is not a valid link and is not keyboard reachable, making these suggestions inaccessible to keyboard users.

issuesFound.fix: Line 460: .btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-3); }

issuesFound.title: touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"] width 28px, height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button is 28x28px, which is below the 44px recommended minimum. Users with motor impairments may mis-tap.
issuesFound.fix: Line 556: #site-search button[type="submit"] { width: 44px; height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] width 20px, height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs are 20x20px, below the 24px minimum. Users with limited fine motor control may have difficulty selecting them.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1016: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; }

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a min-height 24px
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have a min-height of 24px, which may be below the 44px recommended minimum touch target. The links lack sufficient padding to compensate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1056: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3); }

issuesFound.title: visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses properties that make content visible (position: static, width: auto, clip: auto). Screen reader users will not hear this content, while sighted users will see it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 307: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has a transform animation on hover, but the reduced-motion media query block (line 640) is empty. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience this animation, which can cause discomfort.
issuesFound.fix: Line 640: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; box-shadow: var(--shadow-2xl); } }

issuesFound.title: negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body rule sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses text and can reduce readability for users with dyslexia, low vision, or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 100: body { word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: -0.05em; }

issuesFound.title: link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: All link underlines are removed globally (text-decoration: none). Links are distinguished only by colour, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 for users with colour blindness or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 118: a { text-decoration: underline; }

focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav toggle function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.toggle(), .open(), and .close() functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not set aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen-reader users will not know the menu's expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: In the mobileNav module, within the open() function add: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true); within the close() function add: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions show/hide dropdown panels but do not set aria-expanded on the associated trigger button. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 for interactive controls.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance function add: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true); In _closeInstance function add: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The setPeriod function changes the active billing period but does not set aria-pressed on the period toggle buttons. Screen-reader users cannot perceive which period is selected.
issuesFound.fix: In the _syncToggleState function, add: setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);

issuesFound.title: Carousel play/pause button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The play() and pause() functions toggle auto-play but do not update aria-pressed on the play button. Users cannot know the current play state.
issuesFound.fix: In the play() function add: setAttr(_playBtn, ARIA.PRESSED, true); In the pause() function add: setAttr(_playBtn, ARIA.PRESSED, false);

issuesFound.title: Product filter updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The applyFilters function updates visible product count and grid but does not announce the change to screen readers. Users with visual impairments may not know the filter results.
issuesFound.fix: After _updateCountBadge call, add: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied?.(s.activeCategory) || `Showing ${totalMatches} products`);

issuesFound.title: Search results updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The _fetchAndRender function updates search suggestions but does not announce the count to screen readers. Users cannot know when results are available.
issuesFound.fix: In the _fetchAndRender function, after rendering results, add: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.searchResultsFound?.(results.length, query) || `${results.length} suggestions found`);

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors do not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The _renderResult function marks fields invalid with CSS classes but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input element. Screen readers won't announce the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: In _renderResult, when result.valid is false, add: setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, true); when valid, add: setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, false);

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialized on page load for mobile nav
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded set on page load. Screen-reader users cannot know the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: In the mobileNav.init function, after getting _trigger, add: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialized on page load for dropdowns
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons do not have aria-expanded set on page load. Screen-reader users cannot know the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: In the dropdownMenu.register function, after setting aria-controls, add: setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: Dynamically hidden dropdown panel not removed from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance function hides the panel with hidden attribute but does not set aria-hidden="true". The panel may remain in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In _closeInstance, add: setAttr(inst.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, true); In _openInstance, add: setAttr(inst.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, false);

issuesFound.title: Dynamically hidden mobile nav drawer not removed from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The close() function hides the drawer but does not set aria-hidden="true". The drawer may remain in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In the close() function, add: setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, true); In the open() function, add: setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, false);

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The setPeriod function changes prices but does not announce the new period to screen readers. Users with visual impairments may not know the change occurred.
issuesFound.fix: In setPeriod, after updating the period, add: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual || 'Switched to annual billing');

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The open() and close() functions expand/collapse answers but do not announce the state change to screen readers. Users cannot know if content expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: In the open() function, add: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqOpened?.(item.question) || 'Answer expanded'); In close(), add: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqClosed?.(item.question) || 'Answer collapsed');

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element (theme toggle) missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The theme toggle buttons use click events but lack keydown handlers for Enter and Space. Keyboard users cannot activate the toggle.
issuesFound.fix: In the init function, add event listener for keydown on buttons with data-theme-toggle, checking for Enter/Space and calling set or toggle.

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element (scroll-to-top button) missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button uses click event but lacks keydown handler for Enter and Space. Keyboard users cannot activate the scroll.
issuesFound.fix: In the init function, add: _btn.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) _onClick(e); });

issuesFound.title: Auto-playing carousel without user-controllable pause
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel auto-plays on init without a user-controllable pause mechanism that persists across page loads. This may violate WCAG 2.2.2.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the play/pause state is saved to localStorage and restored on init, and provide a persistent pause control.

issuesFound.title: Form submission success not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The _showSuccess function updates the DOM but does not announce the success to screen readers. Users with visual impairments may not know the form submitted.
issuesFound.fix: In _showSuccess, add: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.contactSuccess || 'Your message has been sent.');

issuesFound.title: Newsletter subscription success not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The _showSuccess function updates the DOM but does not announce the success to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: In _showSuccess, add: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.newsletterSuccess || 'You are now subscribed.');

issuesFound.title: Product filter chip removal not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Clicking a filter chip removes the filter but does not announce the change to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: In _onChipClick, after removing the filter, add: liveRegion.announce(`Removed filter ${label}`);

issuesFound.title: Search input clear button not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The clear() function empties the search input but does not announce the clearing to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: In the clear() function, add: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.searchClearing || 'Search cleared');

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open`, `mobileNav.close`, and `mobileNav.toggle` functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but never update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not know if the menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1048: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` (inside open), Line 1061: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` (inside close), Line 1075: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, _open ? 'false' : 'true');` (inside toggle)

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load

issuesFound.fix: Line 1113: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dynamically-hidden element not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` and `mobileNav.close` functions toggle the visibility of the `_overlay` element using the `hidden` attribute and CSS classes, but they do not update `aria-hidden`. This can lead to inconsistent behavior in some screen reader/browser combinations.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1046: `_overlay.setAttribute(ARIA.HIDDEN, 'false');`, Line 1063: `_overlay.setAttribute(ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: user action result not announced via aria-live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` and `mobileNav.close` functions change the visible state of the navigation menu, but there is no corresponding write to a live region to announce "Navigation menu opened" or "closed".
issuesFound.fix: Line 1048: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen);`, Line 1061: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed);`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global `:focus-visible` rule removes the default focus outline (`outline: none`) and only adds an offset, leaving keyboard users without any visible focus indicator. This affects all interactive elements that receive focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 298: `:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: The rule `:focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: none; }` strips the focus outline for mouse‑only interactions, providing no visual cue for users navigating with a mouse or touch.
issuesFound.fix: Line 303: `:focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 2px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons lose their focus ring because `.btn:focus-visible` sets `outline: none` without any alternative styling, making it impossible for keyboard users to see which button is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 352: `.btn:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 2px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement inside forced‑colors media query
issuesFound.explanation: Within `@media (forced-colors: active)`, the `:focus-visible` rule again removes the outline (`outline: none`) and provides only an offset, which is ignored in high‑contrast mode, leaving no focus indicator for users of forced‑colors.
issuesFound.fix: Line 361: `:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid Highlight; outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant `.btn-sm` defines `min-height: 20px`, which is smaller than the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum of 44 × 44 px, making it difficult for users with limited motor control to tap accurately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 424: `.btn-sm { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2-5) var(--space-3-5); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: Icon‑only buttons (`.btn-icon`) have `min-width: 20px; min-height: 20px`, both below the required 44 px target area, creating a small tap target for keyboard and touch users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 440: `.btn-icon { min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2-5); }`

issuesFound.title: Negative word‑spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` rule sets `word-spacing: -0.2em;`, which compresses space between words and can make text harder to read for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 158: `word-spacing: normal;`

issuesFound.title: Visually‑hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` class uses `position: static; width: auto; height: auto; overflow: visible; clip: auto;`, which does **not** hide the element visually while keeping it accessible; instead it leaves the content in the accessibility tree and visible to sighted users, defeating its purpose.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative visual cue
issuesFound.explanation: The rule `a { text-decoration: none; }` removes the default underline from all links, and no other visual differentiator (border, background, or font‑weight change) is provided, making links indistinguishable from surrounding text for low‑vision users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 184: `a { text-decoration: underline; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn` defines a transition that includes `transform` (`transition: ... transform var(--transition-fast)`) and the `:active` state applies `transform: translateY(1px);`. No `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` rule disables this movement, causing unwanted animation for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: `.hero-image img` transitions `transform` and rotates on hover, but the `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block for this selector is empty, so the animation runs for users who have requested reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .feature-card:hover
issuesFound.explanation: `.feature-card:hover` applies `transform: translateY(-2px);` with a transition, but there is no reduced‑motion override, causing motion for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .pricing-card:hover
issuesFound.explanation: `.pricing-card:hover` includes `transform: translateY(-2px);` with a transition, lacking a reduced‑motion override.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .pricing-card--featured:hover
issuesFound.explanation: `.pricing-card--featured:hover` applies `transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01);` with a transition, but no reduced‑motion handling is provided.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .product-card:hover
issuesFound.explanation: `.product-card:hover` uses `transform: translateY(-3px);` with a transition, without a reduced‑motion override, causing unwanted motion for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover
issuesFound.explanation: `.hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover` applies `transform: translateY(-1px);` with a transition, but no reduced‑motion override exists.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav trigger missing `aria-controls`
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`SELECTORS.navToggle`) toggles the navigation drawer but never receives an `aria-controls` attribute that points to the drawer element, so screen‑reader users cannot discover the relationship.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav trigger missing initial `aria‑expanded` state
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger’s `aria-expanded` attribute is only set when the drawer is opened/closed; it is absent on page load, violating the requirement that the state be reflected before any interaction.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu trigger does not update `aria‑expanded` on open/close
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is shown, the trigger button’s `aria-expanded` value is never changed, leaving assistive technologies unaware of the expanded state.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu trigger missing initial `aria‑expanded` state
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger is created without an explicit `aria-expanded` value, so its default state is undefined for AT.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger does not set `aria‑expanded="true"` when opened
issuesFound.explanation: Opening a FAQ item updates visual classes but never updates the trigger’s `aria-expanded` to `true`, breaking the ARIA disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix:
```javascript
function open(item, announceToSR) {
  …
  setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); // ← add
  …
}
```

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing initial `aria‑expanded` state
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button is created without an `aria-expanded` attribute, so its collapsed state is not announced.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons do not update `aria‑pressed` when toggled
issuesFound.explanation: The UI shows the active period via CSS classes, but the buttons never receive an `aria‑pressed` value, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the current selection.

issuesFound.title: Pricing “billing‑toggle” switch lacks `aria‑pressed` updates
issuesFound.explanation: The switch toggles between monthly and yearly billing but never updates its `aria‑pressed` state, violating the ARIA switch pattern.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel period buttons do not update `aria‑selected` when active
issuesFound.explanation: The visual “active” state is reflected only by a CSS class; the corresponding `aria-selected` attribute is never set, so screen‑reader users cannot tell which slide is current.

issuesFound.title: Search suggestion list items do not update `aria‑selected` when highlighted
issuesFound.explanation: Keyboard navigation changes the visual “active” class but never updates each item’s `aria-selected` state, breaking the ARIA listbox pattern.

issuesFound.title: Search suggestion list items lack initial `aria‑selected="false"` attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When the list is first rendered, each `<li role="option">` has no `aria-selected` value, leaving the default undefined for AT.
issuesFound.fix:
```javascript
function _itemHtml(item, index, query) {
  return `
    <li
      id="suggestion-item-${index}"
      role="option"
      class="suggestion__item"
      data-index="${index}"
      data-suggestion-id="${escapeHtml(item.id)}"
      aria-selected="false"               // ← add
    > …`;
}
```

fpTitles: Testimonials carousel period buttons do not update `aria‑selected` when active
Search suggestion list items lack initial `aria‑selected="false"` attribute

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded on its trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`.nav-toggle`) opens and closes the mobile drawer, but the code never sets or updates the `aria‑expanded` attribute on the button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the drawer’s state.
issuesFound.fix:
After changing the drawer state, call `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', _open ? 'true' : 'false');` (or use `setExpanded(_trigger, _open, _drawer)`).

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button lacks aria‑pressed / aria‑controls attributes on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button never receives `aria‑pressed` (or `aria‑checked`) to indicate its toggle state, nor `aria‑controls` pointing to the drawer element, violating ARIA best practice for toggle controls.
issuesFound.fix:
In `init()`, after locating `_trigger`, add: `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id); setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'false');` and update it in `open()`/`close()`.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close does not update aria‑expanded on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is opened, the trigger button’s visual state changes, but the script never updates `aria‑expanded` to reflect the open/closed state, so assistive technologies cannot convey the change.
issuesFound.fix:
Inside `_openInstance()` and `_closeInstance()`, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` and `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` respectively.

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons do not update aria‑pressed when toggled
issuesFound.explanation: The period selector buttons receive a visual “active” class, but the ARIA state `aria‑pressed` is never set, leaving the current billing period ambiguous to assistive tech.
issuesFound.fix:
After toggling the class, call `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', btn.classList.contains(CSS.active) ? 'true' : 'false');`.

---

issuesFound.title: Simple billing‑toggle switch lacks aria‑checked attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The on/off switch for billing period is given `role="switch"` but never receives the required `aria‑checked` state, breaking the ARIA switch pattern.
issuesFound.fix:
After creating `_toggleEl`, set `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', 'false');` and update it in `_onToggleClick()` to reflect the current period.

---

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field fails validation the script adds error text and CSS classes but never sets `aria-invalid="true"` (or removes it on success). Assistive technologies cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_renderResult()`, replace `srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl);` with `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` and on success remove the attribute.

---

issuesFound.title: aria‑expanded not initialised on mobile‑nav trigger at page load
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button starts with no `aria-expanded` attribute, so its initial closed state is not conveyed.
issuesFound.fix:
In `init()`, after locating `_trigger`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

---

issuesFound.title: aria‑expanded not initialised on dropdown triggers at page load
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons are created without an initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute.
issuesFound.fix:
In `register()`, after ensuring `aria-haspopup`, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

---

issuesFound.title: aria‑expanded not initialised on FAQ question buttons at page load
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ triggers lack an initial `aria-expanded` value, so their collapsed state is not announced.
issuesFound.fix:
After creating `trigger`, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

---

issuesFound.title: Hidden panels (mobile drawer, dropdown panels, FAQ panels) lack aria‑hidden when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Elements that become invisible are only given the `hidden` attribute; without `aria-hidden="true"` assistive technologies may still expose them.
issuesFound.fix:
In each hide routine, add `setAttr(element, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` and in the show routine remove the attribute.

---

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period change is not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the user switches between monthly/annual pricing, the UI updates but no ARIA live region is used to announce the change, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the new price context.
issuesFound.fix:
After updating the UI, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || 'Billing period switched to ' + BILLING_PERIODS[period].label);`.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ panel open/close is not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing an FAQ answer updates the DOM but does not provide an audible cue via a live region, so users of assistive technology may miss the change.
issuesFound.fix:
After toggling, add `liveRegion.announce(item.open ? STRINGS.faqOpened(item.question) : STRINGS.faqClosed(item.question));`.

---

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter result count update is not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, the badge showing the number of matching products changes, but no live region is used to announce the new count.
issuesFound.fix:
After setting the badge text, call `liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count===1?'':'s'} found`);`.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like a border or box-shadow in the same rule-block. outline-offset alone does not render a visible ring when outline width is 0. This removes keyboard focus visibility for all elements.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px but lacks a visible outline, border, or box-shadow. This hides focus state for all buttons using this class.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input:focus-visible rule sets outline: none and outline-offset: 0 without a visible alternative. While :focus has a box-shadow, :focus-visible overrides it with none, hiding focus for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The rule for .form-group input:focus-visible (covering input, select, textarea) sets outline: none without a visible replacement. This removes focus visibility for form controls.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have outline: none on focus-visible without a visible alternative. This makes it impossible for keyboard users to identify the focused link.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn min-height 32px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets min-height: 32px, which is below the recommended 44px minimum touch target size (WCAG 2.5.8). This makes buttons difficult to activate on touch devices.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px absolute minimum and 44px recommended size. Small buttons are hard to tap accurately.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #search-input height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input has an explicit height: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size. Users may struggle to activate the input on touch screens.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] width/height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has explicit width: 20px and height: 20px, below the 24px minimum. Checkboxes are difficult to toggle on touch devices.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a min-height 24px
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have min-height: 24px, which is below the 44px recommended size. Links may be hard to tap on mobile.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The rule #site-search label.sr-only sets display: none, which removes the label from the accessibility tree entirely. Screen reader users will not hear the label for the search input.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses transform and transition for animation, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at Line 408 is empty. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see the animation.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img uses transform: rotateY/rotateX and transition, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at Line 722 is empty. Motion is not suppressed for users who request it.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which condenses text and reduces readability, especially for users with cognitive disabilities or dyslexia.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a foreground #94a3b8 / background #555555

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function adds the open class to the drawer and removes hidden from the overlay, but it fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the navigation menu is now expanded.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close function removes the open class and hides the overlay, but it fails to set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the navigation menu is now collapsed.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav overlay visibility not synced with aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module toggles the hidden attribute on the overlay element but does not update aria-hidden. When the overlay is visually hidden, it remains in the accessibility tree, potentially confusing screen reader users.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close actions not announced to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close functions emit custom events but do not announce the state change (e.g., "Navigation menu opened") to the shared live region. Screen reader users are not notified of the navigation state change.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function removes the hidden attribute from the panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not know the dropdown is open.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _closeInstance does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._closeInstance function hides the panel but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not know the dropdown is closed.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu register does not initialize aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register function sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on the trigger. The initial state is ambiguous to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch _open does not update aria-expanded on input
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch._open function sets the internal open state but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the search input element. The combobox state is not reflected to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch _close does not update aria-expanded on input
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch._close function resets the internal state but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the search input element. The combobox state is not reflected to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch suggestion list visibility not synced with aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestionListRenderer.show and hide functions toggle the hidden attribute but do not update aria-hidden on the listbox. The listbox may remain accessible to screen readers when visually hidden.

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters count update not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters function updates the product count badge text but does not announce the change to a live region. Screen reader users are not informed of the new result count after filtering.

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle switch does not update aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing module assigns role="switch" to the toggle element but _syncToggleState only toggles a CSS class. It fails to update aria-checked, which is required for the switch role.

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod change not announced to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod function updates prices and savings messages but does not announce the billing period change (e.g., "Prices switched to annual billing") to the live region. Screen reader users are not notified of the context change.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2115: emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', { from: prev, to: period });
  liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not set aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function sets the item state to open and removes hidden from the panel, but it does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets it to false, creating an inconsistent state.

issuesFound.title: srUtils markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid function removes the error reference from aria-describedby but does not remove or reset the aria-invalid attribute on the field. The field remains marked as invalid to assistive technologies even when valid.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator bind does not initialize aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator.bind function attaches validation listeners but does not initialize aria-invalid="false" on the field. If the field is valid on load, the absence of the attribute is acceptable, but explicit initialization ensures consistent state reporting.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: This selector removes the focus outline for mouse users by setting outline: none without providing any alternative visual indicator. While this targets mouse focus, it provides no fallback and combined with the :focus-visible rule leaves no focus indication for any interaction method.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on forced-colors :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, the :focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only outline-offset, removing the focus indicator in high-contrast mode. Users relying on Windows High Contrast Mode lose all focus visibility since box-shadow and borders are also suppressed in this mode.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This utility class inherits outline: none from the global :focus-visible rule and only adjusts outline-offset to -3px without restoring a visible outline. Keyboard users cannot see focus on elements using this class.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This utility class sets only outline-color while inheriting outline: none from the global rule, resulting in no visible focus outline. Setting only the color property does not restore the outline width or style needed for visibility.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button sets outline: none with only outline-offset in its focus-visible state, providing no visible focus indicator. Keyboard users navigating the header cannot see when this button receives focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button removes the focus outline with outline: none and provides only outline-offset, leaving no visible focus indicator. Keyboard users cannot see when the search button is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links set outline: none with only outline-offset, removing all visible focus indication. Keyboard users cannot track focus position within the account navigation region.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .feature-card a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Feature card links set outline: none with only outline-offset, providing no visible focus ring despite being critical interactive elements. Keyboard users cannot see which card link has focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons set outline: none with only outline-offset, removing visible focus indication. Keyboard users cannot see when the monthly/annual toggle buttons receive focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .table-wrapper:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollable table wrapper sets outline: none with only outline-offset, providing no visible focus ring despite being keyboard-focusable for scrolling. Keyboard users cannot see when focus enters the table region.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Form inputs set outline: none with outline-offset: 0, completely removing focus visibility. Despite having a box-shadow on :focus, the :focus-visible state specifically removes all indication, conflicting with the intended focus-visible styling.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs set outline: none with only outline-offset, providing no visible focus ring. Keyboard users cannot see when checkboxes receive focus, making form navigation difficult.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .footer-brand a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer brand links set outline: none with only outline-offset, removing visible focus indication. Keyboard users cannot see when focus moves to footer links.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .social-links a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links set outline: none with only outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator. Keyboard users cannot see when social link icons receive focus.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets min-height to 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating these small touch targets.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: Icon-only buttons set both min-width and min-height to 20px, falling below the 24px minimum touch target size. These small square buttons are difficult for users with tremors or limited dexterity to activate.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs explicitly size at 20px width and height, below the 24px minimum touch target requirement. Users with motor disabilities may struggle to select these small form controls.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on .nav-toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button sets minimum dimensions to 28px, meeting the 24px minimum but falling below the 44px recommended target size. Users with motor impairments may find this mobile menu button difficult to activate reliably.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on #nav-menu > li > a
issuesFound.explanation: Primary navigation links set min-height to 28px, which meets the 24px minimum but is smaller than the 44px recommended touch target size. Users with limited motor control may have difficulty selecting these navigation items.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on [id$="-submenu"] li a
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown submenu links set min-height to 28px, below the recommended 44px touch target size. Users navigating expanded menus may struggle to activate these smaller links reliably.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button sets explicit dimensions of 28px by 28px, meeting the 24px minimum but below the 44px recommendation. This small icon button may be difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on .filter-tabs button
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons set min-height to 28px, below the recommended 44px touch target size. Users interacting with the product filter may have difficulty reliably selecting these category buttons.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on .pricing-toggle button
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons set min-height to 28px, below the recommended 44px size. Users attempting to switch between monthly and annual billing views may find these controls difficult to activate.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class incorrectly uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto, making content visible on screen instead of hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to assistive technologies. This breaks the intended functionality of providing screen-reader-only text.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link uses transition: transform for spatial movement when appearing, but no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule disables this animation. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort from the sliding motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons define transition properties including transform for spatial movement during interactions, but lack a prefers-reduced-motion override to disable these animations. Users sensitive to motion may experience adverse effects from the button movement.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn:active
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons apply transform: translateY(1px) when active (pressed), creating spatial movement without a prefers-reduced-motion override. Users with vestibular sensitivities may find this downward shift uncomfortable or disorienting.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image applies transform transitions for 3D rotation effects on hover, but the existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 787 is empty and fails to disable these animations. issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and visible dimensions instead of absolute positioning and clipping, making it visible on screen but also potentially hiding it from screen readers depending on browser implementation. This breaks the intended functionality of providing content only to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The button includes transform in its transition property but has no prefers-reduced-motion override to disable this animation for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn:active
issuesFound.explanation: The button active state uses transform: translateY(1px) without a prefers-reduced-motion override to disable the animation for users sensitive to motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero CTA button
issuesFound.explanation: The hero CTA button includes transform in its transition without a prefers-reduced-motion override to disable the animation for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero CTA hover
issuesFound.explanation: The hero CTA button hover state uses transform: translateY(-1px) without a prefers-reduced-motion override.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image includes transform in its transition without a prefers-reduced-motion override to disable the 3D rotation animation for users sensitive to motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image hover
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image hover state uses transform: perspective(...) without a prefers-reduced-motion override to disable the animation.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words and can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on blockquote text
issuesFound.explanation: Blockquote text uses var(--colour-text-secondary) which is #aaaaaa against a white background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.9:1, failing the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.

Line 274: 
  color: var(--colour-neutral-700);

issuesFound.explanation: Code elements use var(--colour-brand-secondary) which is #bb88ee against var(--colour-neutral-100) which is #f1f5f9, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.5:1, failing the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 229: 
  color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover);

issuesFound.explanation: The search input placeholder uses var(--colour-neutral-400) which is #94a3b8 against #f8fafc (neutral-50), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.7:1, failing WCAG AA.

issuesFound.explanation: The danger button uses var(--colour-error) which is #ee9999 as background with white text, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 1.9:1, failing the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.

issuesFound.explanation: Product card body text uses var(--colour-text-secondary) which is #aaaaaa against white, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.9:1, failing WCAG AA.

issuesFound.explanation: The pricing section header paragraph uses var(--colour-text-secondary) which is #aaaaaa against white, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.9:1, failing WCAG AA.

issuesFound.explanation: The global anchor selector sets text-decoration: none without providing a non-colour visual differentiator such as underline on hover or font-weight change, making it difficult for colour-blind users to distinguish links from surrounding text.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative on account navigation
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links explicitly set text-decoration: none and only provide a colour change on hover, which is insufficient for colour-blind users to distinguish links from text.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The open function adds CSS classes to show the mobile navigation drawer but does not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to "true". Screen readers will not announce that the menu is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2225: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` immediately after the line `_open = true;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The close function removes CSS classes to hide the drawer but does not set aria-expanded to "false" on the trigger button. This leaves screen readers believing the menu is still open.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2240: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` immediately after the line `_open = false;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation initializes, the trigger button does not have aria-expanded set to "false" to reflect that the menu is closed by default.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2285: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after the line `_initialised = true;` (or after the event listeners are attached)

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button does not have an aria-controls attribute linking it to the drawer element, preventing screen readers from identifying which element it controls.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2285: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'));` after the drawer element is confirmed to exist

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function missing focus return
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile menu closes, keyboard focus is not returned to the trigger button that opened it, potentially stranding keyboard users at the bottom of the page or in an inactive region.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2250: Add `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();` immediately before the `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', ...)` call

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function reveals the dropdown panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button, so screen readers do not announce the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2375: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _closeInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function hides the dropdown panel but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button, leaving the accessibility tree in an incorrect state.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2390: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` immediately after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu register missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a new dropdown, the trigger button is not initialized with aria-expanded="false" to reflect that the panel is hidden by default.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2452: Add `if (!trigger.getAttribute(ARIA.EXPANDED)) setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after the line setting aria-controls

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _closeInstance missing focus return
issuesFound.explanation: The function accepts a returnFocus parameter but never implements it; focus is not returned to the trigger button when the dropdown closes, stranding keyboard users inside the closed panel or at the end of the document.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2395: Add `if (returnFocus && inst.trigger) inst.trigger.focus();` immediately before the emit call

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: While the close function sets aria-expanded="false", the open function fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button when expanding an FAQ item.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~4155: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch _open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox opens the suggestion panel but does not update aria-expanded to "true" on the input element, preventing screen readers from announcing that suggestions are available.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3265: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` immediately after `_searchState.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch _close missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox closes the suggestion panel but does not set aria-expanded to "false" on the input element.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3275: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` immediately after `_searchState.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch init missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The search input element does not have aria-expanded initialized to "false" when the page loads, even though the suggestion panel is initially closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3325: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after the line `_initialised = true;` (or after _inputEl is confirmed)

issuesFound.title: pricing _syncToggleState missing aria-pressed update
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/annual) update their visual active state via CSS classes but do not update the aria-pressed attribute to reflect which button is pressed.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3845: Add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);` inside the forEach loop, after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: pricing init missing aria-pressed initialization
issuesFound.explanation: When the pricing module initializes, the period toggle buttons do not have aria-pressed set to reflect the initially selected billing period.

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When marking a form field as invalid, the function adds aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true", so screen readers do not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2032: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` immediately after the addDescribedBy call

issuesFound.title: srUtils markValid missing aria-invalid clear
issuesFound.explanation: When clearing the invalid state from a field, the function removes the error message from aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid to "false" or remove the attribute, leaving the field marked as invalid to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2050: Add `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` or `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` at the end of the function

issuesFound.title: productFilter init missing aria-pressed on view mode buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The grid/list view toggle buttons are not initialized with aria-pressed to reflect the current view mode stored in state.

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied and the product count changes, screen reader users are not notified of the new result count because there is no aria-live announcement.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3505: Add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);` after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period changes, the price updates visually but screen readers are not notified of the new prices or the period change via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3830: Add `liveRegion.announce(`Prices updated to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);` after the plan cards are updated

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open and close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ items are expanded or collapsed, screen readers receive no confirmation of the state change because the functions do not write to a live region.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~4155 (open): Add `liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} expanded`);`
  Fix: Line ~4185 (close): Add `liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} collapsed`);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open and close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the mobile navigation menu changes the visible content significantly but does not announce the state change to screen readers via a live region.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~2225 (open): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');`
  Fix: Line ~2245 (close): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance and _closeInstance missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus changes the visible content but does not announce the state change to screen readers, leaving users unaware that content has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~2375 (_openInstance): Add `liveRegion.announce(`Menu opened`);`
  Fix: Line ~2395 (_closeInstance): Add `liveRegion.announce(`Menu closed`);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls and initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is queried but never programmatically associated with the drawer panel via aria-controls, and the initial aria-expanded="false" is not set on page load. Assistive technologies cannot identify which element is controlled by the button.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a `_trap` variable for focus trapping but never initializes or activates it when the drawer opens. Keyboard users can tab out of the drawer into the background page, violating WCAG 2.4.7 and 2.4.3.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu _openInstance and _closeInstance functions show and hide the panel using CSS classes and the hidden attribute, but do not update aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce the expanded/collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1960: Inside _openInstance, after `inst.open = true;` add: `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
  Line 2000: Inside _closeInstance, after `inst.open = false;` add: `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a dropdown, the code ensures aria-haspopup and aria-controls are set, but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" for collapsed dropdowns on page load.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close functions missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open() and close() functions update the visual state and CSS classes, and they set item.open property, but they do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the current state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3125: Inside open(), after `item.open = true;` add: `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
  Line 3170: Inside close(), after `item.open = false;` add: `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The site search input acts as a combobox but the _open and _close functions do not update aria-expanded to indicate whether the suggestion listbox is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2312: Inside _open(), add: `if (_inputEl) setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
  Line 2322: Inside _close(), add: `if (_inputEl) setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch missing initial aria-expanded on combobox
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is assigned role="combobox" but aria-expanded is not initialized to "false" on page load when the suggestion list is hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2275: After `suggestionListRenderer.mount(_inputEl);` add: `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play/pause button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel play/pause toggle button cycles between two states but does not update aria-pressed to indicate the current state (true when playing, false when paused).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3465: Inside play(), add: `if (_playBtn) setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', 'true');`
  Line 3475: Inside pause(), add: `if (_playBtn) setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: pricing period toggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons ([data-period]) indicate active state via CSS classes but do not set aria-pressed="true" on the active button and aria-pressed="false" on inactive buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2578: Inside the _periodBtns.forEach loop, add: `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When marking a field as invalid, the function updates aria-describedby to link the error message but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Screen readers cannot identify the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1375: At the end of markInvalid(), add: `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid clearing
issuesFound.explanation: When clearing the invalid state, the function removes the error from aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute, leaving stale invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1405: At the end of markValid(), add: `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing live region announcement for open/close
issuesFound.explanation: When the navigation menu opens or closes, no announcement is made to screen readers to confirm the state change, leaving users uncertain if the action succeeded.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1837: Inside open(), add: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');`
  Line 1880: Inside close(), add: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled between monthly and annual, the price updates visually but the change is not announced to screen readers, leaving users unaware of the price change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2540: After `_syncToggleState(period);` add: `liveRegion.announce(\`Billing period changed to \${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label}\`);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menus open or close, no announcement is made to screen readers to indicate the menu state change.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1965: Inside _openInstance, add: `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');`
  Line 2005: Inside _closeInstance, add: `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing aria-hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer is hidden using CSS classes but remains in the accessibility tree because aria-hidden="true" is not set when closed. Screen readers may still access the hidden content.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1870: Inside close(), after removing classes add: `if (_drawer) setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`
  Line 1835: Inside open(), after adding classes add: `if (_drawer) setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer toggle function adds the open CSS class and removes the hidden attribute from the drawer, but fails to synchronise the aria-expanded state on the trigger button. Screen readers will not announce the expanded state of the navigation menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2150: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` immediately after `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The close function hides the drawer by adding the hidden attribute and removing CSS classes, but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. This leaves screen readers believing the menu is still expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2169: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` immediately after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When opening a dropdown panel, the function removes the hidden attribute and adds CSS classes, but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (inst.trigger). This violates the ARIA disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2284: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` immediately after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When closing a dropdown panel, the function adds the hidden attribute after a delay, but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button, leaving the accessibility tree out of sync.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2297: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` immediately after `if (!inst.open) inst.panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._open does not update aria-expanded on combobox
issuesFound.explanation: The search suggestion panel is shown by removing the hidden attribute, but the combobox input element (_inputEl) does not have its aria-expanded attribute set to "true". Screen readers cannot tell the suggestion list is visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3124: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` immediately after `_searchState.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._close does not update aria-expanded on combobox
issuesFound.explanation: When closing the suggestion panel, the function hides the listbox but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the combobox input, leaving the expanded state stale in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3132: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` immediately after `_searchState.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: pricing._syncToggleState does not update aria-pressed on billing buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/quarterly/annual) indicate active state via CSS classes only. They lack aria-pressed attributes to communicate the pressed state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3798: Inside the forEach loop, add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));` immediately after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When marking a field as invalid, the function adds aria-describedby pointing to the error message but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Screen readers cannot identify the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1370: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` immediately after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When clearing validation errors, the function removes aria-describedby but does not remove aria-invalid or set it to "false", leaving the field marked as invalid in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1378: Add `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` at the end of the function (inside the else block after removing aria-describedby, or after the if-else block).

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters does not announce filter results
issuesFound.explanation: After applying filters and updating the visible product cards, no aria-live announcement is made to inform screen reader users how many results are now showing or that the filter has been applied.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3420: After `emit(_gridEl, 'filter:applied', ...);`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterApplied ? STRINGS.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) : \`Showing \${totalMatches} products\`);`

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod does not announce billing period change
issuesFound.explanation: When switching between monthly, quarterly, and annual billing, the price values update visually but no announcement is made to screen reader users about the change or the new prices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3770: After `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', ...);`, add `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || 'Billing changed to {period}').replace('{period}', BILLING_PERIODS[period].label));`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close functions do not announce navigation state
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the mobile navigation drawer changes the visible UI significantly, but no live region announcement confirms the action to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2150: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');` after `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:open', ...);`. Line 2169: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` after `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', ...);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close does not announce dropdown state
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding and collapsing dropdown menus changes the visible UI, but no live region announcement confirms the action for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2284: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` after `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:open', ...);`. Line 2297: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');` after `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:close', ...);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init does not initialise aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When initialising the mobile navigation module, the trigger button is found but aria-expanded is not set to "false" initially, even though the drawer is closed by default.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2208: After `_trigger.addEventListener('keydown', _onTriggerKeydown);`, add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.register does not initialise aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a new dropdown, the trigger button has aria-haspopup and aria-controls set, but aria-expanded is not initialised to "false" even though the panel is hidden by default.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2314: After `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`, add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch.init does not initialise aria-expanded on combobox
issuesFound.explanation: In suggestionListRenderer.mount, the input is configured as a combobox with aria-controls and aria-haspopup, but aria-expanded is not set to "false" initially while the suggestion list is hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3040: After `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');`, add `setAttr(input, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: stickyHeader hide/show does not announce header visibility change
issuesFound.explanation: The sticky header hides on scroll down and shows on scroll up, which removes/adds viewport space, but no announcement is made to screen reader users about this visibility change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2394: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.headerHidden || 'Header hidden');` after `emit(_header, 'header:hide');`. Line 2406: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.headerShown || 'Header visible');` after `emit(_header, 'header:show');`

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips._onChipClick does not announce filter removal
issuesFound.explanation: When a user removes a filter chip, the product grid updates to show more items, but no live region announcement confirms the filter was removed or that the results have updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3614: After the switch statement and before `render();`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterRemoved || 'Filter removed');`

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent._showBanner does not announce banner display
issuesFound.explanation: The cookie consent banner appears dynamically and traps focus, but no assertive announcement is made to inform screen reader users that a cookie consent dialog has appeared requiring their attention.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4704: After `emit(document, 'cookieconsent:shown', {});`, add `liveRegion.announceAssertive(STRINGS.cookieBannerShown || 'Cookie consent banner shown. Please review your preferences.');`
