issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation section trigger buttons missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons that expand/collapse sub-sections in the mobile navigation control the isExpanded state but lack aria-expanded to communicate the state to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1240: Add aria-expanded={isExpanded} to the button element: <button ... aria-expanded={isExpanded} ...>

issuesFound.title: Navigation dropdown chevron icon not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron character (▲/▼) provides visual indication of the dropdown state but is redundant for screen readers once aria-expanded is added. It should be hidden to prevent redundant announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1077: Add aria-hidden="true" to the chevron span: ...

issuesFound.title: Theme toggle button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton component requires an aria-label prop according to its TypeScript interface, but ThemeToggle does not provide one. The button contains only an emoji icon, leaving screen reader users without context about its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1284: Add aria-label={LABELS[theme]} to the IconButton: <IconButton aria-label={LABELS[theme]} ...>...</IconButton>

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton requires an aria-label prop but receives only aria-controls. Without a label, screen reader users cannot identify the button's purpose (previous slide).
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1694: Add aria-label="Previous testimonial" to the IconButton: <IconButton aria-label="Previous testimonial" ...>...</IconButton>

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel next button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton requires an aria-label prop but receives only aria-controls. Without a label, screen reader users cannot identify the button's purpose (next slide).
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1699: Add aria-label="Next testimonial" to the IconButton: <IconButton aria-label="Next testimonial" ...>...</IconButton>

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form error message not connected to input
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput has aria-describedby="newsletter-email-error", but FormGroup is not passed errorId="newsletter-email-error", so the error message element is never rendered with that ID. This creates a broken ARIA reference that leaves screen reader users unaware of validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1844: Add errorId="newsletter-email-error" and errorMessage={email.state.error} to FormGroup props. Ensure ErrorMessage component receives id="newsletter-email-error".

issuesFound.title: Contact form error messages not connected to inputs
issuesFound.explanation: The form inputs have aria-describedby referencing error IDs (e.g., 'contact-name-error'), but FormGroup components are not passed the corresponding errorId props, so error message elements are not rendered with those IDs. This creates broken ARIA references for all four form fields.
issuesFound.fix: For each FormGroup, add errorId prop matching the aria-describedby value used in the corresponding TextInput/Textarea (e.g., errorId="contact-name-error", errorId="contact-email-error", etc.).

issuesFound.title: Billing period toggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The buttons toggle between monthly and annual billing periods and show visual active state, but lack aria-pressed to communicate the selected state to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1574: Add aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} to the monthly button. Line ~1581: Add aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} to the annual button.

issuesFound.title: Skip navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The page contains multiple nav elements (SkipLinks, DesktopNav, MobileNav, Footer). The SkipLinks nav lacks an aria-label, making it impossible for screen reader users to distinguish it from other navigation regions.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~555: Add aria-label="Skip links" to the nav element: <nav aria-label="Skip links">...</nav>

issuesFound.title: Desktop navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The page contains multiple nav elements. The DesktopNav nav lacks an aria-label, making it impossible for screen reader users to distinguish it from other navigation regions.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1089: Add aria-label="Desktop navigation" to the nav element: <nav aria-label="Desktop navigation">...</nav>

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation panel landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The MobileNav component contains a nav element that lacks an aria-label. While the dialog has a label, the internal nav should also be labelled to distinguish it from other navigation regions.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1220: Add aria-label="Mobile navigation" to the nav element: <nav aria-label="Mobile navigation">...</nav>

issuesFound.title: Hero section landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The section element lacks aria-labelledby or aria-label. It contains an h1 with id="hero-heading" that should be referenced to create a labelled landmark region.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1344: Add aria-labelledby="hero-heading" to the section element: <section aria-labelledby="hero-heading">...</section>

issuesFound.title: Statistics section landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The section element lacks aria-labelledby or aria-label. It contains an h2 with id="stats-heading" that should be referenced to create a labelled landmark region.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1408: Add aria-labelledby="stats-heading" to the section element: <section aria-labelledby="stats-heading">...</section>

issuesFound.title: Pricing plan cards missing accessible names
issuesFound.explanation: The article element representing each pricing plan lacks aria-labelledby. It contains an h3 with id={headingId} that should be referenced to provide an accessible name for the card.

issuesFound.title: Desktop navigation current page link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The active navigation link uses a visual class 'nav-link--current' when item.current is true but lacks aria-current="page", so screen reader users cannot identify it as the current page.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1104: Add aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} to the anchor element: <a ... aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} ...>

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation current page link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The active navigation link uses a visual class 'mobile-nav__link--current' when item.current is true but lacks aria-current="page", so screen reader users cannot identify it as the current page.

issuesFound.title: Required field marker not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The asterisk (*) marking required fields is not hidden with aria-hidden="true", causing screen readers to announce "star" or "asterisk" redundantly alongside the "(required)" text provided by VisuallyHidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~946: Add aria-hidden="true" to the asterisk span: *

issuesFound.title: Button loading spinner not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The loading spinner span has no aria-hidden attribute. Since the button already provides a VisuallyHidden loading label, the spinner is decorative and should be hidden from assistive technology to avoid redundant announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~893: Add aria-hidden="true" to the spinner span: <span className="btn-spinner" aria-hidden="true" />

issuesFound.title: Button loading state not communicated with aria-busy
issuesFound.explanation: When isLoading is true, the button content changes but this loading state is not communicated to assistive technology. The button should have aria-busy="true" to indicate it is processing.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~882: Add aria-busy={isLoading} to the button element: <button ... aria-busy={isLoading} ...>

issuesFound.title: Modal backdrop click target missing button semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The modal overlay div has an onClick handler to close the modal but lacks role="button", tabIndex={0}, and keyboard event handlers (onKeyDown). Keyboard-only users cannot activate this click target to close the modal.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1924: Add role="button", tabIndex={0}, and onKeyDown handler for Enter/Space keys, or change the element to a <button> styled as a full-screen overlay.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation backdrop click target missing button semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation backdrop div has an onClick handler to close the navigation but lacks role="button", tabIndex={0}, and keyboard event handlers. Keyboard-only users cannot activate this click target.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1208: Add role="button", tabIndex={0}, and onKeyDown handler for Enter/Space keys, or change to a <button> element.

issuesFound.title: Integration marquee clone not hidden from assistive technology

Line ~1487: Add aria-hidden="true" to the second (clone) ul element:
    ...

issuesFound.fix: Line ~598: Add aria-hidden="true" to the spans containing the star characters: ...
